'''Mike W. Barr''' (born [[May 30]], [[1950s#1952|1952]]) is an American writer of [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] and science fiction novels, who co-wrote several issues of the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' series with [[Michael A. Stackpole|Michael Stackpole]].

==Biography==
'''Mike W. Barr''' was born on [[May 30]], [[1950s|1952]]. His introduction into comics writing came in [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC Comics]] [[Wikipedia:Detective Comics|''Detective Comics'']] #444, for which he wrote an 8-page back-up mystery feature starring the [[Wikipedia:Elongated Man|Elongated Man]] in late 1974/early 1975. His credits then skip ahead four years, when he wrote a further couple of back-up features for ''Detective Comics'' and ''House of Mystery'' as well as an issue of [[Wikipedia:Captain America|''Captain America'']] (#241) for [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] in January 1980. By mid-1980 he was writing regularly for both DC and Marvel, and in 1981 penned stories for [[Wikipedia:Marvel Team-Up|''Marvel Team-Up'']] and [[Wikipedia:Mystery in Space|''Mystery in Space'']] as well as various Batman and [[Wikipedia:Green Lantern|Green Lantern]] titles, among others.

[[Wikipedia:Legion of Super-Heroes|''Legion of Super-Heroes'']] #277 (July 1981) saw him take on editorial duties at DC, while writing issues of Marvel's [[Wikipedia:Star Trek|Star Trek]] comic, for whom he created the native American character Ensign Bearclaw and a pacifist Klingon named Konom. In December 1982, he debuted the first issue of one of the earliest maxi-series, [[Wikipedia:Camelot 3000|Camelot 3000]], with art by [[Wikipedia:Brian Bolland|Brian Bolland]], one of few titles Bolland provided full artwork on.

In August 1983, he created what may well be his most enduring work, the monthly title [[Wikipedia:Batman and the Outsiders|''Batman and the Outsiders'']], which he also wrote for every issue of the first series, and its Baxter paper spinoff, [[Wikipedia:The Outsiders|''The Outsiders'']].

His other comics work includes [[Wikipedia:Mantra (comic)|''Mantra'']] and [[Wikipedia:Maze Agency|''Maze Agency'']] as well as the 1987 hardcover book [[Wikipedia:Batman: Son of the Demon|''Batman: Son of the Demon'']], proceeds from which reputedly "restored DC Comics to first place in sales after fifteen years." This title, and Barr's work on Batman with artist [[Wikipedia:Alan Davis|Alan Davis]] have been cited by [[Wikipedia:Grant Morrison|Grant Morrison]] as key inspirations for his recent (2006) run on the [[Wikipedia:Batman (comic book)|''Batman'']] title.

Most recently he wrote a two-part story for the pages of DC's [[Wikipedia:JLA#JLA:_Classified|''JLA: Classified'']] (#47-48, Jan-Feb 2008), returned to the Outsiders with ''Outsiders: Five of a Kind -- Katana/Shazam'' #1 (Oct 2007), contributed to Tokyopop's ''Star Trek: The Manga'', and relaunched ''Maze Agency'' at [[Wikipedia:IDW Publishing|IDW Publishing]].
